messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Bioc Package Browser is down
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioc team,

The Bioc package home pages link out to a code browser, which is a very useful resource to be able to refer external users or reviewers to. The code browser seems to be down at the moment. Following the link from the edgeR devel home page

  https://code.bioconductor.org/browse/edgeR/devel/

gives the message "404 Not Found, nginx", although repeated tries sometimes get the alternative message "This site can?t be reached".

Thanks
Gordon
https://gksmyth.github.io

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] ggplot2 v4.0.0 related errors; related to ggbio and maybe other packages
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] ggplot2 v4.0.0 related errors; related to ggbio and maybe other packages
messages.body_plain:
Hi bioc-devel (cc Michael Lawrence),

As some of you might be aware of, ggplot2 just had a major version
release described in detail at
https://www.tidyverse.org/blog/2025/09/ggplot2-4-0-0/.

I see that several of my Bioconductor packages that make
visualizations with ggplot2 (either directly or under the hood through
other packages such as ggbio) are now failing in bioc-release and
bioc-devel.

* spatialLIBD: https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.22/data-experiment-LATEST/spatialLIBD/nebbiolo2-checksrc.html
seems like can fix by changing a unit test. I actually just did by
making the unit test more versatile so it works with older versions of
ggplot2 too: https://github.com/LieberInstitute/spatialLIBD/commit/910497195987e09ebef745437056a09b53c7a464.
* regionReport:
https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.22/bioc-LATEST/regionReport/nebbiolo2-checksrc.html.
Failing because ggbio is failing.
* derfinderPlot: uses regionReport, which uses ggbio.
https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.22/bioc-LATEST/derfinderPlot/nebbiolo2-buildsrc.html

Michael seems to have recently (< 24 hrs ago) been made aware of this
issue at https://github.com/lawremi/ggbio/issues/175.

But well, other Bioconductor packages might also be having related
ggplot2 v4.0.0 issues, which is why I'm sending this email to
bioc-devel. Just as a heads up!

Best,
Leo


Leonardo Collado Torres, Ph. D.
Investigator, LIEBER INSTITUTE for BRAIN DEVELOPMENT
Assistant Professor, Department of Biostatistics
Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health
855 N. Wolfe St., Room 382
Baltimore, MD 21205
lcolladotor.github.io
lcolladotor at gmail.com

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] MultiRNAflow error reported
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] MultiRNAflow error reported
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor Core Team,

I received few days ago an email reporting problems in my package
(
https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.21/bioc-LATEST/MultiRNAflow/nebbiolo1-buildsrc.html
).
I was suprised because I did not do any changes and it appears to be a
latex problem from the vignette (Rnw file) when it tries to build the pdf.

The error is : "LaTeX errors: ! Dimension too large.
   \fb at put@frame ...p \ifdim \dimen@ >\ht \@tempboxa ..."

However I can not understand where the error is (precisely which line in
the Rnw vignette), so I srtuggle to find a solution. I would like to know
- if you have any idea about how to locate the problem
- if this problem has already been reported
- if a solution has already been proposed.

Thank you in advance for your help.

Sincerely yours,

Rodolphe Loubaton

messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Change maintainer email ID of betaHMM
messages.body_plain:
To change the maintainer email in the package, you would simply push the change in the DESCRIPTION along with a valid version bump.

See the following:
https://contributions.bioconductor.org/git-version-control.html

You will likely have to activate your BiocCredentials account and add ssh keys for push access.  I will send an email off list with instructions.

Please also consider updating your profiles on support.bioconductor.org.

Cheers,



Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

order to avoid deprecation.

Let us know if you have concerns or need help with this.

Best,

H.

packages EWCE and ewceData. The current maintainer, Alan Murphy (

*Email:* hdash.work at gmail.com

I am registered with BiocCredentials. Alan is copied here for confirmation.
I will also be updating the DESCRIPTION of these packages soon.

Thank you for your assistance.

Best regards,
Hiru

--

Research Assistant in Computational Genomics
UK Dementia Research Institute at Imperial College London

Dear BioConductor Community,

I am writing to report a technical issue I have encountered when attempting
to load ExperimentHub object "EH5376" from the ewceData package.

*Problem Description:*
When attempting to load the specified resource, I consistently receive a "C
stack usage too close to the limit" error. This issue is NOT present in the
release R and Bioc versions.

*Reprex:*
(bioc-devel, R 2025-08-22 r88678)

*Method 1: Loading via data package*

> ewceData::ctd()see ?ewceData and browseVignettes('ewceData') for documentationloading from cacheError loading resource. attempting to re-downloaddownloading 1 resourcesretrieving 1 resourceloading from cacheError: failed to load resource  name: EH5376  title: ctd  reason: C stack usage 7957312 is too close to the limit


*Method 2: Loading directly with ExperimentHub*

> hub <- ExperimentHub::ExperimentHub()snapshotDate(): 2025-09-22> hub[["EH5376"]]see ?ewceData and browseVignettes('ewceData') for documentationloading from cacheError loading resource. attempting to re-downloaddownloading 1 resourcesretrieving 1 resourceloading from cacheError: failed to load resource  name: EH5376  title: ctd  reason: C stack usage 7953728 is too close to the limit


*Additional Context:*
I have verified that the package functions correctly when using the
*bioconductor_docker:RELEASE_3_19* image. No modifications have been made
to the data or codebase ever since.

This error is also affecting the BioConductor build checks for packages
that depend on this data resource. Specifically, the issue manifests in
R-devel, BioC 3.22 build results, as evidenced in the EWCE package check
results:
https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/devel/bioc-LATEST/EWCE/nebbiolo2-buildsrc.html

I have prepared a complete traceback, attached below.

I would be most grateful for any assistance you could provide in resolving
this issue.

Yours sincerely,
Hiru

--

*Hiranyamaya (Hiru) Dash* (he/him)

UK Dementia Research Institute at Imperial College London

Hiru

messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] PRONE Build Errors
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

my package PRONE, available on Bioconductor, is currently failing to build under Bioconductor versions 3.21 and 3.22 due to an incompatibility between ComplexUpset and ggplot2 (? 4.4.0). A related issue has already been reported here: krassowski/complex-upset#213 <https://github.com/krassowski/complex-upset/issues/213>.

Here is the bild/check reports of PRONE for 3.21: https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/release/bioc-LATEST/PRONE/kunpeng2-buildsrc.html

I would appreciate advice on possible workarounds to ensure that PRONE remains accessible both in Bioconductor 3.21 and in the upcoming 3.22 release (October).
Best,
Lis Arend
?
Lis Arend
PhD Student

E-mail: lis.arend at tum.de
Web:http://daisybio.de

TUM School of Life Sciences
Technical University of Munich

85354 Freising
Germany

messages.body_plain:
The Bioconductor 3.22 Release is tentatively scheduled for Wednesday October 29th.

Packages submitted to the new package submission queue at this time are a first come first serve basis.  Reviewers are volunteer and review packages as time permits.  Submitting now will not guarantee inclusion into 3.22. It must pass the submission process including an official review and be accepted by October 22. Packages not meeting this deadline will still be reviewed and will be accepted and published into Bioconductor devel for 3.23 inclusion.

Please remember,  The Bioconductor 3.21 branch will be frozen Monday   October 13th.   After that date, no changes will be permitted ever on that branch.

The deadline for devel Bioconductor 3.22 for packages to pass R CMD build and R CMD check is October  27th.  While you will still be able to make commits past this date,  This ensures any changes pushed to git.bioconductor.org are reflected in at least one build report before the devel branch will be copied to a release 3.22 branch.

If there are any questions or concerns please reach out at bioc-devel at r-project.org

Cheers,



Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263
